tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand the project's purpose: building an IELTS and Academic English Anki deck from dictionary sources. It provides the roadmap for the scraper, semantic review, deck construction, design, and release layers that the following stops connect.

tour.title: Operating Contract
tour.description: AGENTS.md and CONTEXT.md define the project's canonical vocabulary, artifact ownership, and release rules. Read them together before changing code: they explain why reviewed registries, deterministic outputs, and the established EAVM identity are treated as contracts rather than incidental implementation details.

tour.title: Production Pipeline
tour.description: The pipeline orchestrator is the runtime spine of the project, moving from scrape through example audio, build, validation, packaging, and verified Anki import. This is the best code-level map of how the independently maintained layers become a release.

tour.title: Dictionary Ingestion
tour.description: Oxford and Cambridge modules turn source pages and fixtures into structured vocabulary records, while merge.py reconciles their evidence and metadata. These modules establish the raw definitions, examples, audio references, and collocation evidence that later review stages consume.

tour.title: Reviewed Collocations
tour.description: The collocation audit is the review boundary for dictionary collocation candidates: it fingerprints coverage, records decisions and provenance, validates the ledger, and promotes a deterministic registry. Treating this as an authority prevents scraper labels from silently becoming production card content.

tour.title: Semantic Registry Build
tour.description: registry_build.py combines the reviewed Card, Semantic, and Collocation registries into the final note payload. It resolves card identity, aligns senses and Vietnamese glosses, and carries source markers forward so each published field remains traceable.

tour.title: Audio And Media
tour.description: The audio gates validate dictionary and content-addressed example media before publication, while the build keeps references deterministic and compatible with Anki's manual-play controls. This boundary is where remote TTS variability is separated from the reproducible note build.

tour.title: Card Design Source
tour.description: design/index.html is the visual source of truth for the EAVM card's zone-2 CSS and interactive preview. The packaged templates and styling are checked against it, so changes to rendering must preserve selector contracts and the Recognition/Production card model.

tour.title: Packaging Provenance
tour.description: The packaging and provenance modules bind the generated APKG to its exact inputs, templates, registries, media set, and tool versions. Release guards use this binding to reject stale or unverifiable artifacts before they cross the publication boundary.

tour.title: Verified Anki Import
tour.description: anki_import_command.py performs the live AnkiConnect import and proves the result with media-byte checks, note identity coverage, and a post-import APKG export. This final stop explains why the workflow never edits collection.anki2 directly and why a verified receipt is part of a release.
